facts.text: The modern English word 'sock' is derived from the Old English word 'socc', meaning 'light slipper'
The Latin word 'soccus' described a light, low-heeled shoe worn by Roman comic actors
The foot can produce over 0.25 US pints (0.12 L) of perspiration per day, making it one of the heaviest sweat producers in the body
In ancient times, socks were made from leather or matted animal hair
Machine-knit socks were first produced in the late 16th century
Because socks were time-consuming to manufacture in preindustrial times, they were long used only by the rich
The poor historically wore footwraps — simple cloths wrapped around the feet — instead of socks
Footwraps remained in use in Eastern European armies until the end of the 20th century
According to the Greek poet Hesiod, Ancient Greeks in the 8th century BC wore socks called 'piloi', made from matted animal hair
Around the 2nd century AD, Romans began sewing fabrics together to make fitted socks called 'udones'
By the 5th century AD, socks called 'puttees' were worn by holy people in Europe to symbolize purity
During the Middle Ages, socks became tight, brightly colored cloth coverings for the lower leg
Medieval socks had no elastic band, so garters were placed over stockings to prevent them from falling down
Fort Payne, Alabama is regarded as the 'sock capital of the world'
Nearly half of all socks manufactured in the early 21st century were made in Fort Payne, Alabama
The Fort Payne sock industry employed about 7,000 workers at its peak around the year 2000
The township of Datang in Zhuji, Zhejiang Province, China is known as 'Sock City'
Datang currently produces 8 billion pairs of socks each year
Datang, China has surpassed Fort Payne, Alabama as the top global sock producer
Liner socks are worn underneath another sock specifically to prevent blisters
The odor of smelly socks is a mixture of ammonia, fatty acids (particularly isovaleric acid), and lactic acid
Odorous socks are a strong attractant for mosquitoes and have been used to help control their behavior
The smell associated with smelly socks also naturally occurs in dairy products, cheeses, sausages, and fish sauce
Smelly sock odor appearing in building air treatment systems is known as 'dirty socks syndrome'
Knee-high and thigh-high socks were widely worn by both boys and girls during the late 19th and early 20th centuries
Socks can be made from silk, bamboo, linen, cashmere, or mohair for added softness
Merino wool retains its shape better when blended with other materials
No-show and low-cut socks are designed to create the appearance of bare feet when wearing shoes
Colored socks are used in sports uniforms to help distinguish teams when only players' legs are visible
Fort Payne still contributes roughly a quarter of global sock output despite losing the top position to Datang
